questions.question: Explain the difference between iterative and concurrent servers. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Iterative servers handle one client at a time, blocking until completion
Concurrent servers handle multiple clients simultaneously using forks/threads/multiplexing
Choose iterative for simple, quick transactions
Choose concurrent for long-running services with multiple clients
Iterative is simpler but has poor scalability

questions.question: Describe the role of the sockaddr_in structure in socket programming. List at least four important fields.
keyPoints: sockaddr_in is the IPv4 socket address structure
Contains sin_family (address family, AF_INET)
Contains sin_port (port number in network byte order)
Contains sin_addr (IP address in network byte order)
sin_zero padding for compatibility with sockaddr

questions.question: What is the purpose of the listen() system call? Explain the backlog parameter and its significance.
keyPoints: listen() marks socket as passive (server) socket
Transforms active socket to listening state
backlog specifies maximum pending connection queue length
If queue full, new connections refused
Typical values: 5-128 depending on expected load

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in establishing a TCP connection between a client and server.
keyPoints: Server: socket(), bind(), listen(), accept()
Client: socket(), connect()
Three-way handshake: SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK
Server gets new socket from accept()
Both sides can now use send()/recv()

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using UDP compared to TCP?
keyPoints: UDP advantages: lower overhead, faster, suitable for real-time
UDP disadvantages: no reliability, no ordering, potential packet loss
No connection setup overhead
Applications: DNS, video streaming, VoIP
Need application-level reliability if required

questions.question: Write a complete TCP echo server program in C using socket programming functions. Include error handling and explain each step.
keyPoints: Create socket with socket()
Bind to address with bind()
Listen with listen()
Accept connections with accept()
Fork child process for each client
Child handles echo, then exits
Parent closes client socket, continues accepting
Use appropriate headers and error checking

questions.question: Design a concurrent TCP server using fork(). Explain how zombie processes are prevented and how the server handles multiple clients simultaneously.
keyPoints: Parent calls accept(), obtains client socket
Parent forks child process
Child handles client communication
Parent closes client socket, continues listening
Use signal(SIGCHLD, SIG_IGN) or wait() to prevent zombies
SO_REUSEADDR for quick restart
Explain file descriptor inheritance and closure

questions.question: Compare and contrast select() and poll() for I/O multiplexing in Unix network programming. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Both enable monitoring multiple file descriptors
select() uses fd_set, limited to FD_SETSIZE (typically 1024)
poll() uses pollfd array, no fixed limit
select() can be less efficient with many descriptors
poll() provides more uniform interface
Both block until descriptor ready or timeout
select() is older, poll() more flexible
epoll() (Linux) better for large-scale servers
